plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and meet the single-name mandate.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF risk, aligning with capital preservation.
Increase QQQI to 25% for more income and broad market exposure.
Buy MSFT at 10% to add a top-quality technology name.
This plan prioritizes capital preservation and reduces overall portfolio risk.
conservative.read: This conservative plan targets around 1.5% per month, focusing on capital preservation and lower drawdown. It significantly reduces leverage and concentration, but growth potential is limited.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the concentration mandate and reduce overall exposure.
Sell SOXL entirely as it is below its stop and a leveraged ETF.
Trim SPAL to 2% to reduce leveraged ETF exposure while keeping a small position.
Add to CRWD, increasing its allocation to 18% given its strong uptrend.
Buy NVDA at 10% as a strong, high-scoring scanner setup to enhance growth.
balanced.read: This balanced plan aims for approximately 2.5% per month, prioritizing risk-first growth. It balances broad market exposure with quality tech, accepting moderate risk for growth.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the single-name concentration mandate.
Sell SOXL entirely as it is below its stop, maintaining strict stop discipline.
Increase CRWD to 20% to lean into a strong uptrending AI name.
Buy NVDA at 15% and AVGO at 10%, leveraging the highest-scoring scanner setups.
This plan uses leverage and concentration to target higher growth, with strict stops.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets around 4% per month, aiming for stretched growth by concentrating in high-scoring setups and using leverage. Be aware of significantly higher drawdown potential and the daily decay/rebalancing of leveraged ETFs.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet concentration limits and free up capital.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure, aligning with income strategy.
Increase QQQI to 30% to maximize exposure to income-generating ETFs.
Buy XLE at 10% to diversify into a potentially income-friendly sector.
This plan prioritizes steady monthly distributions and low drawdown over growth.
income.read: This income plan targets around 1% per month, focusing on steady distributions and capital preservation. Distributions are variable, and NAV can still decline in market downturns; this is not a savings account.
